images.altText: A crowd of people hold Iran flags. One holds a sign that says Down with Israel

articles.title: Donald Trump threatens to hit Iran 'extremely hard' over next few weeks

children.content: In his first national address since the start of the war, President Donald Trump says the US will hit Iran hard over the next two to three weeks, and tells allies they need to secure the Strait of Hormuz if they want to calm oil markets.

image.altText: Donald Trump sits at his desk in the oval office looking into the camera.

articles.title: Trump's speech was significant for what he did not say

children.content: The US president's national address was largely a cobble-together of past talking points and Truth Social posts. Perhaps the most notable takeaways relate to what went unmentioned.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands behind a wooden door.

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

children.content: Iran's armed forces spokesperson says the war will continue until the "enemies surrender", hours after US President Donald Trump threatened to hit Iran "extremely hard". Follow live.

items.title: Iran's military vows 'crushing' attacks against US and Israel after Trump threats

updates.title: Opposition leader gives Albanese three things to do over fuel crisis
Angus Taylor responds to prime minister's national address
Iran's military says war will only end when 'enemies surrender'

items.title: 'Worth the risk': Aussies ignore travel warnings to keep flying via Middle East

image.altText: The front of a grounded Emirates plane.

children.content: Australians are choosing cheaper airfares over safer flights to get to Europe by transiting through the Middle East, despite federal government warnings.

items.title: Albanese's Scott Morrison moment came this week

children.content: This week in Canberra, the prime minister had a message for Australians, his treasurer laid the groundwork for the upcoming federal budget and Angus Taylor's car was stolen.

image.altText: Men in suits speak at a press conference

items.title: Angus Taylor says 'put Australia first' during national address

children.content: Angus Taylor has issued a return serve to the prime minister's national address by urging people to "put Australia first" while accusing the government of providing a lack of clarity about the ongoing fuel crisis.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: Sandra's brain is failing her and she lives in 'extreme pain' from her vaccination injury

children.content: For years these vaccine-injured Australians have been trying to get compensation from a 'torturous' government compensation scheme.

gallery.altText: A woman and man are seated at a table, looking at a document.
A woman sitting on a sofa.

thumbnail.altText: A woman and man are seated at a table, looking at a document.
A woman sitting on a sofa.

items.title: PM announces gambling ad ban in stadiums, during school pick-up

children.content: Gambling ads to be partially restricted on television and radio, with opt-out rules for online promotions and a ban in stadiums and on jerseys under reforms unveiled by Labor.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: One Nation wins fourth seat in SA lower house by small margin

children.content: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

image.altText: A woman in One Nation t-shirt, with flyer tucked under one arm, while speaking to a voter

items.title: Anonymous police force completes review into Alice Springs death in custody

children.content: NT police have received the independent use of force review into the death in custody of Kumanjayi White in Alice Springs in 2025.

image.altText: An illustration of an Indigenous man with dark hair and a beard, standing on a red dirt landscape and wearing a yellow t-shirt.

items.title: New pact affirms NZ as Pacific nation's partner of choice after spat over China

children.content: New Zealand and the Cook Islands have mended their relationship with a pact that restores funding to the Pacific island nation.

image.altText: Two ships with blue hulls and white bridges in a harbour with forested mountains in the background.

items.title: Why the Artemis II mission to the Moon is so groundbreaking

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

gallery.altText: Graphics featuring a vector flight path for the Artemis-II mission from the Earth to the moon

thumbnail.altText: Graphics featuring a vector flight path for the Artemis-II mission from the Earth to the moon

items.title: In pictures: Artemis II NASA's first mission to the Moon in decades

image.altText: The NASA Artemis II SLS rocket launching.

children.content: For the first time in more than 50 years NASA has sent astronauts around the Moon, marking a major milestone in a new era of space exploration.

items.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Activist ship deliberately collides with trawler as 'krill wars' escalate

children.content: An environmental activist group’s ship has deliberately collided with a large fishing trawler in Antarctic waters. The activists say it was a "gentle nudge" but the trawler's owner says a line has been crossed.

image.altText: View from the deck of a ship as it crashes into another ship.

items.title: My kid wants to run with me. When can they start?

children.content: If you enjoy running and your child has shown interest in coming along, you might be wondering when they're old enough to start. Experts say there are a few ways you can determine if they're ready.

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Here is the key to getting through The Super Mario Galaxy Movie

children.content: Even with downgraded expectations, the Nintendo sequel is little more than Easter eggs and bright colours. But that won't stop it from filling cinemas.

image.altText: A shot from Super Mario Galaxy Movie featuring Mario, Luigi, Peach and Yoshi

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia

children.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

image.altText: A close up of a plastic dinosaur.

items.title: Weather to deliver Easter treat, but another cyclone looms next week

children.content: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

gallery.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

thumbnail.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: The PM said 'enjoy your Easter'. Here's what you should know first about fuel

children.content: Thanks to fuel supply concerns your Easter trip may not look like normal. Here's what you need to know before hitting the road.

image.altText: A line of fuel bowsers with a sorry sign.

items.title: Australia to attend global meeting on Strait of Hormuz — without the US

children.content: Australia will join a virtual meeting of 35 countries to discuss plans to reopen and secure the Strait of Hormuz, and ease the oil shock rocking global markets.

image.altText: Penny Wong

items.title: Powerful magnitude-7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia, tsunami warning issued

children.content: The quake, which hit early today, had a depth of 35 kilometres and its epicentre was 127km west-north-west of Ternate, Indonesia, the US Geological Survey said.

image.altText: An aerial view of islands around Raja Ampat in east Indonesia's West Papua region

items.title: Class action filed against Kmart alleges 'systemic' wage underpayment

children.content: Kmart managers have alleged they were routinely required to work "well beyond" their rostered hours without receiving overtime rates, penalty rates, or prescribed allowances.

image.altText: A generic Kmart sign on a shopping centre.

items.title: Thousands lost to cancellations as fuel crisis bites tourism sector

children.content: Tourism businesses say they're bearing the brunt of surging fuel prices, with some losing thousands to booking cancellations and extra operational costs, while others field back-to-back calls from anxious tourists about fuel availability.

items.title: Bondi gunman fails to have family's identities suppressed for 40 years

children.content: Lawyers for Bondi gunman Naveed Akram fail in a bid to have the identities of his mother, brother and sister protected by a decades-long court suppression order on safety grounds.

image.altText: Naveed Akram

items.title: 'Stressed': Backpackers losing jobs as Exmouth shuts to tourists

children.content: In a WA town long reliant on backpackers, there may be few workers left by the time it is ready to reopen for business.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair looking at camera

items.title: Australian-based Harrods survivor was 'fresh game' for Mohamed Al Fayed

children.content: Jessica tried to keep her distance from Harrods billionaire owner Mohamed Al Fayed, who was known to abuse his female staff, until he spotted her going down an escalator.

items.title: Tszyu ready to revive world title dream with Easter Sunday bout

children.content: Tim Tszyu is promising no more slip-ups while candidly confessing to "falling apart" after relinquishing his world title in the infamous Sebastian Fundora bloodbath in Las Vegas.

image.altText: Australian boxer Tim Tszyu speaks to media during a press conference in Sydney

items.title: Kerr in, but several Matildas stars not part of squad for FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr has been named, but the Matildas will be missing a number of their other big guns when they travel to Kenya for the team's maiden FIFA Series in April.

image.altText: Mary Fowler of Australia during the AFC Women’s Asian Cup dribbling the ball during a match

items.title: Explaining Carlton's infamous second-half fade-outs

children.content: Carlton's horrible second halves have become a costly trend which has the team struggling and Michael Voss fighting for his job. This is what is going wrong after half-time for the Blues.

image.altText: Three Carlton players look puzzled while chatting on the field after a defeat

items.title: States and territories agree to extra fuel excise cut using GST revenue

children.content: Premiers and chief ministers agree to use an estimated $400 million in extra GST revenue to fund a 5.7 per litre cut to the fuel excise, on top of the Commonwealth's 26.3 cent per litre relief.

image.altText: A person fuels up their vehicle at a petrol station close up.

items.title: 160-year-old historic stone cottage for sale for first time in decades

children.content: Originally built as storage for a grand residence, the stone cottage is the last surviving structure, going back to the very foundations of the city.

gallery.altText: Stone brick cottage structure in garden with tall roof and verandah with wooden chair

thumbnail.altText: Stone brick cottage structure in garden with tall roof and verandah with wooden chair

avatar.alt: Woman with blonde brown hair, black top, headshot

items.title: International crypto firm targets uni students despite ASIC ban

children.content: A major cryptocurrency platform is operating like "gambling" in Australia, according to experts, despite new regulatory laws.

image.altText: Two large gold coins bearing engraved "b"s lie on top of a complex financial line-and-bar graph

items.title: One Nation candidate published antisemitic, racist and homophobic posts

children.content: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

image.altText: A collage of screenshots of tweets on top of an image of Tyler Green smiling while posing for a photo.

items.title: DNA links US serial killer Ted Bundy to unsolved 1974 murder

children.content: New DNA testing definitively links infamous serial killer Ted Bundy to the unsolved death of a US teenager in 1974.
